title: set_diversifier_index is a blind overwrite with no monotonicity guard
description: Scenario: a future caller passes a value lower than the current DB diversifier_index (e.g. after a rollback, or a bug in the advance logic). Result: diversifier index moves backward; addresses from already-used DIs are re-issued; address reuse breaks payment unlinkability. The current call site is safe (always increasing due to the found_u128 > start guard), but the function itself does not enforce this. Should be renamed advance_diversifier_to and implement SET WHERE diversifier_index < excluded.diversifier_index, making it physically impossible to regress. Today there is one call site; when a second appears, the next engineer will copy the pattern without noticing the precondition.
